does: This paper develops a practical multi-view fusion pipeline for space object detection in LEO constellations using YOLO-based deep learning detectors. The authors design and evaluate several input representations that combine observations from multiple satellites simultaneously, testing different configurations including RGB and grayscale multi-view inputs on a SOD dataset. Key results demonstrate substantial performance gains: a three-view RGB fusion with YOLOv9-m achieves mAP50 of 0.732 compared to 0.638 for single-view, while the best three-view grayscale configuration improves mAP50 by 36.3 percent, with corresponding improvements in mAP50-95 metrics.
connects: This work builds on established object detection architectures (YOLO family) adapted for the space domain, extending recent advances in multi-view fusion techniques from terrestrial and autonomous driving applications to the specific challenge of orbital collision avoidance. It responds to growing concerns about LEO constellation congestion and space debris tracking, connecting to prior space object detection research while incorporating collaborative sensing principles—the idea that physically distributed observers (multiple satellites) can share information to improve detection reliability. The paper leverages deep learning frameworks proven effective in related vision tasks, bringing these computational methods under the practical constraints of satellite computing hardware.
enables: This approach opens pathways for distributed collaborative space surveillance systems where LEO constellation satellites collectively improve situational awareness through shared observations, reducing individual computational and power burdens while enhancing detection robustness. Future work could explore real-time onboard fusion implementations, dynamic multi-satellite coordination strategies, and integration with existing space traffic monitoring networks. The demonstrated feasibility of multi-view fusion for SOD may enable autonomous collision avoidance systems within constellations, contribute to space debris cataloging efforts, and establish frameworks for inter-satellite communication protocols optimized for safety-critical detection tasks in increasingly congested orbital regions.
